cxu_object.claim: In ranging market regimes the optimal strategy is mean reversion entering long at the lower fifteen percent of the Bollinger band range and short at the upper eighty-five percent with maximum position size and a target of two to three trades per day

supporting_contexts.text: Mean Reversion in ranging: buy at lower Bollinger band (<15% of range), sell at upper (>85%). Take profit when price returns to mean (40-60% zone). Peak performance: +$18 in stable ranging period. Critical failure: pyramiding disaster when price trends down, kept adding at 0% of range and lost -$23.
Regime-adaptive playbook for RANGING: execute mean reversion strategy with max position size. Consensus threshold 75% of signals must agree. Maximum 2-3 entries per day to keep fee drag under $6.

entryLowPct.description: Bollinger band percentile for long entry

entryHighPct.description: Bollinger band percentile for short entry

tpZoneLowPct.description: Lower bound of take-profit zone (mean reversion target)

tpZoneHighPct.description: Upper bound of take-profit zone (mean reversion target)

maxTradesPerDay.description: Maximum number of entries per day in ranging regime

consensusThresholdPct.description: Minimum percentage of signals that must agree before entry

version.change_description: Initial ranging playbook from regime-adaptive strategy results
